title: salix × obtusifolia
intro: Salix × obtusifolia, also known as Willow-leaved White Willow or Bay Willow, is a hybrid plant species that is a cross between the Salix alba (White Willow) and Salix fragilis (Crack Willow). The bark of Salix × obtusifolia contains salicin, a compound with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a valuable medicinal plant. The medicinal properties of Salix × obtusifolia have been used to treat various health issues, including fever reduction, pain relief, and rheumatism, providing relief from symptoms associated with these conditions. Salix × obtusifolia can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and salves, making it accessible for use in traditional medicine.
